reasoning: The prompt tests if the Fed will follow a "cut-pause-cut" sequence for the Sept, Oct, and Dec 2025 FOMC meetings. The September 2025 meeting already resulted in a 0.25% rate cut. However, CME FedWatch data from mid-to-late October 2025 shows a 97-100% market implied probability that the Fed will cut rates again in October 2025. This means the probability of a "pause" in October is incredibly low (0-3%). Consequently, the "cut-pause-cut" sequence is almost entirely priced out by markets, favoring instead a "cut-cut-..." sequence. I am submitting 0.05 as the probability, factoring in the strict 1-3% implied odds of an Oct pause and the minimum probability thresholds.
